observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to address the issue on the balance of previous years’ arrears and conduct thorough evaluation of the account to reconcile the composition of the remaining balance.

1.6 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MAO and MTO to closely coordinate to adjust  the noted discrepancy  of ₱3,824,772.89 and reconcile  their respective cash record balances on a monthly basis; (b) MT to (i) submit to the MAO a list of unclaimed/unreleased checks in her possession every end of the year;  and (ii) endorse all the stale checks on a monthly basis  to the MAO for proper recording; and (b) MA to draw the necessary adjusting entries for the reversion/restoration to the Cash-in-Bank account and appropriate liability/payable accounts  the total amount of unreleased and stale checks to reflect the correct account balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a)  Municipal Accountant to (i)   exert coordinated efforts with the MTO to adopt the perpetual inventory method to establish sound controls over the handling, monitoring and recording of supplies inventory as prescribed under the same Manual; (ii) ensure that procured supplies are  recorded under the appropriate inventories account upon receipt of AIR; and (iii) record supplies consumption based on the SSMI with supporting RIS submitted by the MTO;  (b) MTO and MAO to (i) initiate the preparation and maintenance of stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively to substantiate the reported balance of supplies inventory in the books; and  (c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) conduct semi-annual physical inventory of supplies; and (ii) prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and (iii)  consolidate on a weekly basis the RIS in the SSMI for submission to the MAO for recording of the appropriate supplies expense.

3.10  	We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for the lands owned by the Municipality and (b) Municipal Assessor and/or Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs/Emancipation Patent (EP) of its land properties as soon as possible in accordance with the provisions of Section 39 (2) of PD No. 1445.

4.4 	We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (a.i) exert efforts in reconciling the discrepancy between the GL and PPELC to arrive at  reliable PPE balances; and (a.ii) draw the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify as  Construction in Progress (CIP)-Infrastructure Assets account the progress billings on the Construction of Flood Control System (Daang Dike sa Sapang Tabon, Nagbalon) and to correct the erroneous provision of depreciation expense thereon; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (b.i) maintain and update the Property Cards per category of PPE; (b.ii) submit the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) pursuant to the provisions of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and (b.iii) monitor and safekeep reported unserviceable PPE and prepare the IIRUP and/or WMR to facilitate the disposal thereof pursuant to applicable rules and regulations; and (c) create an inventory committee that will conduct the annual physical count of properties in CY 2022  and every year, thereafter following the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

5.6	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Public Information Office to comply with the mandatory posting of the Report on Fund Utilization of LGSF-FA in the Agency Website and in at least three (3) conspicuous places within the Municipality pursuant to DBM LBC No. 136 dated March 30, 2021.

6.7 	We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials/offices to furnish the Audit Team with copies of Contracts/Purchase Orders and supporting documents, and Notices of Deliveries as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

7.7 	We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to (a) exert coordinated efforts with the implementing offices of the Municipality to adopt strategies to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs amidst the new normal set-up;  (b) improve project planning,  monitoring and strengthen control policies in the utilization of GAD Funds; and (c)  coordinate with the MPDO on the establishment and maintenance of the GAD database which will be used in its gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

8.7 	We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit to the Office of the Auditor the accounts and financial reports of the Municipality; and (b) strictly comply with the provisions of laws, rules, and regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period for the conduct of timely audit.

9.15	We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the attainment of the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality; (b) require the Municipal Development Council and other concerned officials to strictly adhere to the guidelines on project identification and inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of development projects pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (c) direct the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit to the AT the noted lacking documents for auditorial review and verification of the regularity  of disbursements.

10.13	We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRMC to (a) strictly comply with  the  allocation of LDRRMF to  cover the thirty percent (30%) lump-sum allocation for Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the seventy percent (70%) allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery  pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular 2012-002 dated  September 12, 2012 and (b) ensure optimum utilization of the 70% Disaster Prevention and Mitigation, Preparedness, Response, Rehabilitation and Recovery Fund by fully implementing the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP to provide adequate protection to  the constituents  in times of disasters.

11.10    We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit the consolidated Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported by acknowledgement receipts, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, and inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any;(b) coordinate with the MDRRMC in complying with the submission of the required reports to the NDRRMC through the OCD; and (c) request assistance from the MPDO and/or MPIO in adhering to the required posting of the report on the receipt and utilization of donations in the Agency’s website.

12.12 	We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly check the supporting documents on claims relative to traveling expenses especially the RER which should be completely and properly accomplished before payment: and (ii) make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify to the ICT Equipment account the amount of ₱74,500.00; (b) require the officer and/or employee responsible for the payment of the water bill to immediately refund the amount of ₱7,940.80 representing the unnecessary expenses incurred due to  penalty  for late payment of the bill; (c)  instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to the end-user of the  purchased laptop and desktop to establish accountability over these properties; and (d) direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to constantly coordinate with one another regarding the proper accounts to be used in drawing Obligation Request and Journal Entry Voucher so as to prevent misclassification of expenditures in their respective Registries and General Ledgers.

13.26 	We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) comply with the pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations on the remittance of the taxes withheld,  contributions, and loan amortizations within the prescribed period to avoid the incurrence of penalties and  fines; and (ii) coordinate with the BIR, GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC in addressing the issue on the prior years’ arrears due them and conduct thorough evaluation of the accounts to substantiate the composition of the unremitted balances; and (iii) once substantiated, cause the immediate remittance thereof; and (b) AAO in coordination with the ERF Handler to navigate and make use of the Electronic Billing and Collection System (eBCS) of the GSIS in (i) updating the records of Municipal employees for correct remittance of their monthly GSIS contributions within the reglementary period; and (ii) monitoring of unpaid premiums of all the employees and facilitating immediate remittance of the same.

14.4 	We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to exercise diligence in the processing of claims and in evaluating the supporting papers of disbursement vouchers to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (b) responsible and liable persons to immediately effect settlement of the disallowed claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to 

(i)  gather/retrieve all information/records, trace the variance/discrepancy  between the two records of ₱18,424,518.23 and reconcile the balances per accounting  ledger and per cashbooks regularly; and 

(ii) prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the  stale checks  of ₱5,283,860.13 to effect proper adjustments in the  cashbook.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer as the Acting General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to  

(a) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); 
(b) maintain complete Property Cards (PC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), respectively; and 

(c) recognize the misclassified PPE accounts to their appropriate accounts  and utilize the  prescribed account codes in the recording of transactions based on the Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) require the Human Resource and Management Officer  to submit the Office Order or Memorandum signed by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) authorizing the names of personnel in the paid payrolls to physically report for work  during the pandemic; 

(b)  Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees who rendered work during the pandemic stating the dates and the purposes as directed by the LCE, their complete DTRs and other supporting documents; and 
(c)  Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entries in recognizing in the books  the paid COVID-19 Hazard Pay of Job Orders, permanent and casual employees.

We require the: 

(a) BAC Secretariat to submit all the deficient documents of the emergency procurements; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers before allowing payment of  all claims.

We recommended that the LCE: 

(a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of every household to support the SWM program of the Municipality on the proper segregation of wastes;

(b) through the OIC – MENRO provide the necessary support in enjoining the 15 barangays in establishing their own MRF; and 

(c) continue to observe the best practices of SWM by fully utilizing the Liwasang Pangkalikasan for the total protection of the environment and the general welfare of the constituents in harmony with economic development.

We recommended that the LCE require the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to always issue Official Receipts to record the return of unexpended balance of every cash advances; 

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents attached to LRs; and
(c) MSWDO to conduct careful validation thru strict house to house screening in accordance with the requirements of the DSWD Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to 

(a) instruct the GFPS TWG to coordinate efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs; and 

(b) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up, and maintenance of a GAD database which will be the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a)  Bids and Awards Committee secretariat to post the Notice of Award, Contract and Purchase Order in the website of the Municipality and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement; and  

(b)  Municipal Accountant  to be cautious in the use of account titles in recording of transactions in the books and strictly observe the chart of accounts with description provided under the  GAM for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 on the remittance of taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period.

We recommended the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to 

(a) strictly comply with the regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share intact within the prescribed period; and 

(b) coordinate and reconcile with GSIS records with regards to the changes in the salaries of employees, if any.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to faithfully comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE instruct 

(a) the Municipal Accountant to exercise diligence  in the processing of claims and in evaluating the supporting papers of disbursement vouchers  to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and 

(b)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and

(c) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

1.3 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i)  gather/retrieve all information/records, trace  the variance/discrepancy  between the two records of ₱18,424,518.23 and reconcile the balances per accounting  ledger and per cashbooks regularly; and (ii) prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the  stale checks  of ₱5,283,860.13 to effect proper adjustments in the  cashbook.

2.4 	We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer as the Acting General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to  (a) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); (b) maintain complete Property Cards (PC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), respectively; and (c) recognize  the misclassified PPE accounts to their appropriate accounts  and utilize the  prescribed account codes in the recording of transactions based on the Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

3.7 	We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.

4.6	We recommended that the LCE  (a) require the Human Resource and Management Officer  to submit the Office Order or Memorandum signed by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) authorizing the names of personnel in the paid payrolls to physically report for work  during the pandemic; (b)  Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees who rendered work during the pandemic stating the dates and the purposes as directed by the LCE, their complete DTRs and other supporting documents; and (c)  Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entries in recognizing in the books  the paid COVID-19 Hazard Pay of Job Orders, permanent and casual employees.

5.5 	We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) BAC Secretariat to submit all the deficient documents of the emergency procurements; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers before allowing payment of  all claims.

6.5	We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Officer  to (i) submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated  September 12, 2012 to the Office of the Auditor for timely review and evaluation of the same to attain transparency and accountability in the use of LDRRMF; (ii) prepare and submit the Inventory of Disaster Equipment as of year-end; and (iii) maintain an organized inventory-keeping  of DRRM equipment, supplies and materials for the efficient management of the same; (b) Municipal Treasurer  to issue acknowledgement receipt in the prescribed form for all donations in-kind received containing the complete details of the goods donated; and (c) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the procurement of welfare goods and the expenses incurred in the implementation of programs, projects and activities to the appropriate accounts.

7.6 	We recommended that the LCE: (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of every household to support the SWM program of the Municipality on the proper segregation of wastes; (b) through the OIC – MENRO provide the necessary support in enjoining the 15 barangays in establishing their own MRF; and (c) continue to observe the best practices of SWM by fully utilizing  the Liwasang Pangkalikasan for the total protection of the environment and the general welfare of the constituents in harmony with economic development.

8.8 	We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) Municipal Treasurer to always issue Official Receipts to record the return of unexpended balance of cash advances; (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents attached to LRs; and (c) MSWDO to conduct careful validation thru strict house to house screening in accordance with the requirements of the DSWD Memorandum Circular.

9.11	We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the Municipal Development Council to  adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the AIP of the programs, projects and activities of the 20% DF pursuant to JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (b) require the  Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the  awarded infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and  Sec 4.1 of GPPB Circular No. 2020-1;(c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

10.6 	We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to (a) instruct the GFPS TWG to coordinate efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs; and (b) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up, and maintenance of a GAD database which will be the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation of the Municipality.

11.13	We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee secretariat to post the Notice of Award, Contract and Purchase Order in the website of the Municipality and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement; and (b) Municipal Accountant to be cautious in the use of account titles in recording of transactions in the books and strictly observe the chart of accounts with descriptions provided under the  GAM for LGUs, Volume III.

12.5 	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 on the remittance of taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period.

13.6 	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to  (a) strictly comply with the regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share intact within the prescribed period; and (b) coordinate and reconcile with GSIS records with regards to the  changes in the salaries of employees, if any.

14.5 	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to faithfully comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

15.4 	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to address the issue on the balance of previous years’ arrears and conduct thorough evaluation of the account to reconcile the composition of the remaining balance.

16.4 	We recommended that the LCE instruct the (i) Municipal Accountant to exercise diligence  in the processing of claims and in evaluating the supporting papers of disbursement vouchers  to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct (a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) provide the corresponding depreciation for Markets and Police, Military and Security Equipment; (ii) maintain PPELC to substantiate the reported PPE account balances; (iii) draw the appropriate journal entry voucher to correct the misclassified PPE accounts; and (iv) ensure that DVs are supported with complete and properly accomplished documentation prior to payment; and (b) the MTO to maintain PCs to facilitate reconciliation with MAO records.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to use the prescribed form in the preparation of the SEF budget; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the prepared quarterly SEF utilization report to appropriate offices and (ii) ensure completeness of documentary requirements before effecting payments; (c) Local Finance Committee to closely monitor/ limit the expenses within the approved appropriations in order to avoid the incurrence of net loss/deficit; (d) member of the LSB to refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities in which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (e) district supervisor of North District to (i) submit a written explanation on the discrepancies between the number of units of vehicles rented as indicated in the P.O. and in the contracts; and (ii) submit the lacking supporting documents in the payments for rental of vehicles.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Human Resource Management Office HRMO) to (i) strictly observe the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s.2015 with regards to the remuneration of overtime services through compensatory time-off and observance of the  limit on overtime pay per individual employee; and (ii) monitor the accumulated overtime pay of every employee to facilitate compliance with the CSC-DBM regulations; (b) concerned department heads to (i) enforce adequate work supervision and planning to the payment of overtime services more than the appropriated amount; and (ii) ensure that the accomplishment reports of personnel under their supervision are quantified in adherence with the requirement of  COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to refrain from charging ineligible expenses to the LDRRMF; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the conduct of competitive bidding in the procurement of standby goods for stockpiling; (c) MAO to adopt the accrual method of accounting for inventories pursuant to IPSAS No. 12; and (d) the MAO and MTO to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for relief goods/supplies for control and monitoring.

We recommended that the LCE and the MENRO: (a) intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the use of MRF in every barangay, and introduce programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid wastes generated; and (b) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to those barangays which remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the Municipal Development Council to  adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the AIP of the 20% DF pursuant to JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (b) require the  Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure that the  infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (ii) demand from respective contractors the  repair of the defective and damaged portion of the road projects as these are still covered by warranty pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  prior to payment.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) (a) instruct the GFPS TWG to coordinate efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs;  and (b) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up, and maintenance of GAD database which will be the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 on the remittance of taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management (a) strictly comply with the regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share intact within the prescribed period; and (b) coordinate and reconcile with GSIS records with regards to changes in the salaries of employees, if any.
We recommended that Management faithfully comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management address the issue on the balance of previous years’ arrears and conduct thorough evaluation of the account to reconcile the composition of the remaining balance.

We recommended that the (a) Management   instruct the (i) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.
We recommended that Management consolidate all procurement requests and strictly adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement pursuant to the provisions of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the District Supervisor of South District to submit a written explanation on the reimbursement of transportation expenses involving the government vehicle; (b) strictly observe the standard procurement procedure  and attach all  the required documentary requirements  such as sales invoices, official receipts and payrolls to establish the validity and occurrence of government transactions; (c) refrain from purchasing supplies and materials thru reimbursement; and (d) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the supplier.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct (a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) provide the corresponding depreciation for Markets and Police, Military and Security Equipment; (ii) maintain PPELC to substantiate the reported PPE account balances; (iii) draw the appropriate journal entry voucher to correct the misclassified PPE accounts; and (iv) ensure that DVs are supported with complete and properly accomplished documentation prior to payment; and (b) the MTO to maintain PCs to facilitate reconciliation with MAO records.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to use the prescribed form in the preparation of the SEF budget; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the prepared quarterly SEF utilization report to appropriate offices and (ii) ensure completeness of documentary requirements before effecting payments; (c) Local Finance Committee to closely monitor/ limit the expenses within the approved appropriations in order to avoid the incurrence of net loss/deficit; (d) member of the LSB to refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities in which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (e) district supervisor of North District to (i) submit a written explanation on the discrepancies between the number of units of vehicles rented as indicated in the P.O. and in the contracts; and (ii) submit the lacking supporting documents in the payments for rental of vehicles.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Human Resource Management Office HRMO) to (i) strictly observe the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s.2015 with regards to the remuneration of overtime services through compensatory time-off and observance of the  limit on overtime pay per individual employee; and (ii) monitor the accumulated overtime pay of every employee to facilitate compliance with the CSC-DBM regulations; (b) concerned department heads to (i) enforce adequate work supervision and planning to the payment of overtime services more than the appropriated amount; and (ii) ensure that the accomplishment reports of personnel under their supervision are quantified in adherence with the requirement of  COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to refrain from charging ineligible expenses to the LDRRMF; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the conduct of competitive bidding in the procurement of standby goods for stockpiling; (c) MAO to adopt the accrual method of accounting for inventories pursuant to IPSAS No. 12; and (d) the MAO and MTO to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for relief goods/supplies for control and monitoring.

We recommended that the LCE and the MENRO: (a) intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the use of MRF in every barangay, and introduce programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid wastes generated; and (b) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to those barangays which remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the Municipal Development Council to  adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the AIP of the 20% DF pursuant to JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (b) require the  Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure that the  infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (ii) demand from respective contractors the  repair of the defective and damaged portion of the road projects as these are still covered by warranty pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  prior to payment.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) (a) instruct the GFPS TWG to coordinate efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs;  and (b) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up, and maintenance of GAD database which will be the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 on the remittance of taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management (a) strictly comply with the regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share intact within the prescribed period; and (b) coordinate and reconcile with GSIS records with regards to changes in the salaries of employees, if any.
We recommended that Management faithfully comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management address the issue on the balance of previous years’ arrears and conduct thorough evaluation of the account to reconcile the composition of the remaining balance.

We recommended that the (a) Management   instruct the (i) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

We recommended that Management ensure that: (a) cash advances are granted only to designated Disbursing Officer or Special Disbursing Officer of the Municipality; (b) no additional cash advances will be allowed to officials and employees unless the previous cash advance granted to them have been liquidated; and (c) no cash advances of elected officials will be permitted other than for official travel.

We recommended that Management direct the MTO and Accounting offices to: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; (b) maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card for land for easy reconciliation of the account; and (c) prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to correct the erroneous charging of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant to request a monthly report on the receivables of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGUs – PGB and the corresponding Tax Revenue accounts; and (b) request the timely remittance of the share of the Municipality on the Real Property Tax and Amusement Tax collected by the PGB.

We recommended that Management: (a) remit to the source agencies the unexpended balance of the trust funds, and to the Bureau of the Treasury the unused balances of the SEA, KKK, and TUPAD whose source agencies are unknown; and (b) expedite the projects’ implementation to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a)MDRRMO to  prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the  Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; and b)  the Municipal Accountant to i)recognize in the books the financial assistance received as Trust Liability-DRRM and the procurement of Disaster Response and procured equipment as Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account; and ii)prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers ( JEV) to correct the erroneous charging of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO: (a)update the  ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the Municipality;(b)  intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  establishing an MRF in every barangay and  establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the 16  Barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003;  (d) require the private hauler to comply with the minimum requirements for transfer station; and (e)  install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Local Development Council and key officials in the identification of projects in the 20% DF to  adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP)  of development projects and its utilization pursuant  to DILG and DBM  JMC No. 2017-1; b) require the  Municipal Engineer to  i) ensure that the  infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport, ii)  respective contractors to  repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to  the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; and c)  Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  before payment is made.
We recommended that the GFPS: (a) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up and maintenance of GAD database; and (b) strive for full implementation of PPAs.
We recommended that Management faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of PhilHealth monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management remit to the source agencies the unexpended balance of the trust funds, and to the Bureau of the Treasury the unused balances of the SEA, KKK, and TUPAD whose source agencies are unknown.

We recommend that Management establish detailed policy guidelines, concurred by the SB, in the grant of various types of financial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those qualified constituents in crisis situation are extended financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to:  (a) submit to the COA Auditor’s Office the advance copies of the duly signed contracts/purchase orders and the notice of deliveries within the deadline; and (b) provide copies of the approved contracts/purchase orders to the Municipal Treasurer/Acting GSO for monitoring of delivery and inspection.

We recommended that Management ensure that: (a) cash advances are granted only to designated Disbursing Officer or Special Disbursing Officer of the Municipality; (b) no additional cash advances will be allowed to officials and employees unless the previous cash advance granted to them have been liquidated; and (c) no cash advances of elected officials will be permitted other than for official travel.

We recommended that Management direct the MTO and Accounting offices to: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; (b) maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card for land for easy reconciliation of the account; and (c) prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to correct the erroneous charging of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant to request a monthly report on the receivables of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGUs – PGB and the corresponding Tax Revenue accounts; and (b) request the timely remittance of the share of the Municipality on the Real Property Tax and Amusement Tax collected by the PGB.

We recommended that Management: (a) remit to the source agencies the unexpended balance of the trust funds, and to the Bureau of the Treasury the unused balances of the SEA, KKK, and TUPAD whose source agencies are unknown; and (b) expedite the projects’ implementation to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time.

We recommended that Management: (a) remit to the source agencies the unexpended balance of the trust funds, and to the Bureau of the Treasury the unused balances of the SEA, KKK, and TUPAD whose source agencies are unknown; and (b) expedite the projects’ implementation to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a)MDRRMO to  prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the  Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; and b)  the Municipal Accountant to ii)recognize in the books the financial assistance received as Trust Liability-DRRM and the procurement of Disaster Response and procured equipment as Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account; and ii)prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers ( JEV) to correct the erroneous charging of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO: (a)update the  ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the Municipality;(b)  intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  establishing an MRF in every barangay and  establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the 16  Barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003;  (d) require the private hauler to comply with the minimum requirements for transfer station; and (e)  install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that Management consolidate all procurement requests and strictly adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement pursuant to the provisions of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the District Supervisor of South District to submit a written explanation on the reimbursement of transportation expenses involving the government vehicle; (b) strictly observe the standard procurement procedure  and attach all  the required documentary requirements  such as sales invoices, official receipts and payrolls to establish the validity and occurrence of government transactions; (c) refrain from purchasing supplies and materials thru reimbursement; and (d) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Local Development Council and key officials in the identification of projects in the 20% DF to  adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP)  of development projects and its utilization pursuant  to DILG and DBM  JMC No. 2017-1; b) require the  Municipal Engineer to  i) ensure that the  infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport, ii)  respective contractors to  repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to  the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; and c)  Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  before payment is made.
We recommended that the GFPS: (a) coordinate with the Planning Department in setting up and maintenance of GAD database; and (b) strive for full implementation of PPAs.
We recommended that Management faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of PhilHealth monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) analyze, review and monitor all recorded receivables to ensure that only valid ones are recognized; and (ii) ensure that dormant, long outstanding and unsubstantiated accounts are appropriately derecognized; and (b)  prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the write-off of these dormant accounts to pave the way for their derecognition and/or adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Receivables to be derecognized was already reviewed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; (b) require the Inventory Committee to submit the inventory reports; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain subsidiary records for PPE and undertake regular reconciliation of their records; and (d) exert best efforts to make the necessary research for the retrieval of documents supporting the untitled parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation for all properties and equipment was already prepared

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely and necessary interventions, and full implementation of the planned development projects may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; and (b) continue to maximize the use of the 20% DF on priority projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and (b) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and utilize it in accordance with the guidelines on the matter.

We recommended that Management ensure that (a) the applicable laws, rules, and regulations affecting cash advances and disbursement of funds are strictly adhered to; and (b) the cash advances granted to the accountable officers is limited only to their maximum accountability guaranteed by the posted fidelity bond.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Human Resource Management Office to require the submission of an accomplishment report of job orders, casuals, and consultants every month together with the approved DTR; and (b) install tighter internal control of its payroll funds for casual and job order employees by using the automated teller machine (ATM) payroll system.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws; (b) intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  establishing MRF in every barangay; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the 16  component Barangays who have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that Management through the Local School Board (a) give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the JC in the preparation of the SEF budget and (b) stop the practice of improperly charging ineligible expenses to SEF which the DepEd has the mandate to disburse out of its own appropriation.

We recommended that the Management instruct the GAD Focal Point System to (a) instruct the GFPS TWG to coordinate efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs; and (b) to submit all the reports required under MCW.

We recommended that Management (a) instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to issue an Order of Payment for the rental of equipment; (b) Municipal Treasurer to receive all collections from all sources and issue the corresponding official receipt; and (c) all payments be coursed through the proper process.
We recommended that Management faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag – IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of PhilHealth monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with the proper offices on the submission of documents to facilitate the prompt submission of accounts and financial reports.
observations.reason: As of date, the submitted accounts and Financial Reports is only as of September 2017.
