17.4    We recommended that Management continue to comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of PhilHealth monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the  Inventory Committee to give utmost priority to the conduct of  the required annual  physical count of property; (b)  create a General Services Officer (GSO) position who will discharge on a full time basis the property custodianship functions and the receipt, issuance and recording of the procurement and issuance of  supplies and property; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile regularly her records with the Municipal Treasurer.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate disposal of the 14 unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations. Retrieve and determine the actual condition of the four units of unserviceable, unutilized and abandoned solid waste  equipment found at the open dumpsite and take appropriate action to either repair, rehabilitate or dispose them accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The IIRUP and inventory of missing parts have been prepared.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant to analyze, review and monitor all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded and that  dormant, long outstanding and unsubstantiated accounts are  properly  disposed  of and derecognized; and (b)  prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the write-off of these dormant accounts to pave the way for their derecognition and/or adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: On-going review by the Municipal Accountant of all booked receivables before the request for derecognition.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Budget Officer to include in the budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Property Officer in-Charge to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of  insurable property with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.
observations.managementAction:
a) Increased amount of insurance was included in 2018 Budget.

b) On going inventory taking

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) effect the closure and transfer of the bank account being maintained with the  non-government depository bank within one year from the effectivity of Department Circular No. 002-2015 unless a Bureau of Local Government Finance approval has been secured; and (b)  henceforth, comply with DOF Department Circular Nos. 001-2015 and 002-2015 in the deposit of   funds of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: All accounts in private commercial banks are already closed. While Philippine Veterans Bank was declared by as an AGDB in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) stop the granting  of cash advances to DepEd officials and limit the granting of  cash advances only to the Municipality’s designated DO; (ii) demand the immediate liquidation of the unliquidated cash   advances totaling ₱553,800.00 from the concerned officials; and; (iii) desist from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; (b) require the two District Supervisors to (i) submit the approved project proposals for the cash advances granted in September, 2016 totaling ₱373,750.00; (ii) refund the difference between the authorized and actual expenses incurred during the centralized training amounting to ₱70,800.00; and (iii) submit all required supporting travel documents to establish the validity of the travel in Tokyo, Japan or refund the amount spent in case of non-submission of the required documents.
observations.managementAction:
a (i) The granting of cash advance to District Supervisor has been stopped; (ii) cash advance amounting to P553,800.00 has been liquidated.

b (ii and iii) verbally demanded from the concerned officials the liquidation of the P78,000.00 and the submission of the required travel documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Budget Officer and the District Supervisor to stop the practice of charging inappropriate expenses to SEF which the DepEd is mandated to incur and pay out of its own appropriations; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from reimbursing expenses to District Supervisors which are not allowable using SEF.
observations.managementAction: a) and b) Refrained from charging inappropriate expenses to SEF and from reimbursing expenses to District Supervisors

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to adopt public bidding as the general method of procurement and to limit the use of alternative mode of procurement to highly exceptional cases only as provided in the said IRR.
observations.managementAction:
Public bidding has been adopted as the general method of procurement.

No transaction for the year on the purchase of test papers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) BAC officials to  religiously post all bid opportunities in the PhilGEPS website; (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers before allowing payment of claims thereof; and (c) Acting General Servicer Officer to maintain a centralized storage area and render necessary reports of issuances of medicines  duly supported with RIS.
observations.managementAction:
a) Posted of all bid opportunities in the PhilGEPS website; 

(b) Ensured the completeness of supporting documents before allowing payment of claims

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal  Accountant  to desist from charging improper expenditures against the 20% development fund and that projects charged  should  partake of  the nature of investment or capital expenditures; (b)  the Acting  General Service Officer to  ensure that no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with theapproved APP;and (c) Bids and Award  Committee post all  projects to be bidded/awarded  in the PhilGEPS website  in accordance with the pertinent provisions in the 2016  IRR of RA No.9184.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Most of the projects are capital expenditures

(b) There is a approved APP for supplies and equipment.

(c) All bidded projects are posted at the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a)  require the Municipal Accountant to make  charges against  the GAD Fund to only  PPAs that are relevant and will address the identified gender issues and their causes; (b) ensure that the GAD  PPAs respond to the identified gender issues and concerns and that GAD mandates are implemented; (c) conduct gender analysis using the prescribed tools  to ensure that PPAs are gender responsive to  address the prevailing and recurring issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men stakeholders, clients and constituents; and  (d) monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs and prepare the AR in the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction:
a) A GAD plan has been prepared and followed.




d) Implementation of GAD PPAs is being monitored by the focal person.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management  (a) require all users of Motor Vehicles to prepare and maintain the complete files of properly accomplished and duly approved Driver’s  Trip Tickets  pursuant to   Section 361, Volume I of the GAAM to effectively control and monitor the use of Motor Vehicles;  and (b) submit  to the Office of the Auditor the required periodic reports of Monthly Report of Official Travels  and Monthly Report of   Fuel Consumption for the timely evaluation of the reasonableness of the utilization of motor  vehicles and fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction:
a) Trip tickets are prepared and maintained

b) A summary of fuel consumption by each office is attached to the DV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO and the LDRRM Council take the necessary and timely recommendation to the SB for the declaration of a   state of calamity if warranted by the prevailing conditions while providing  immediate response to disaster victims. In case there is a declaration of a state of calamity by the SB, the corresponding lifting under appropriate circumstances thereafter, shall also be issued.
observations.managementAction: Not applicable

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO conduct  actual validation of the families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victims’  profiles containing updated and realistic data to become the basis in next year’s planning  activities to ensure adequate  and appropriate procurement of  relief goods on a timely basis to properly address  the needs of disaster victims.
observations.managementAction: The preparation of the profile of population in high risk areas is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned offices namely the MSWDO and the MDRRMO immediately inform the Municipal Treasurer or his  authorized representative on the existence of donations in-kind to properly acknowledge the receipt thereof. Likewise, proper reporting, accounting, and recording of accountability for donated goods should be made by the respective offices.
observations.managementAction: No donations-in-kind yet

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO and the BAC officials to undertake on a timely basis   the immediate  procurement of the necessary rescue equipment and vehicles  in accordance with the approved AIP for the year.
observations.managementAction: 2 units ambulance already purchased and on service; 1 unit rescue truck already purchased

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) establish a materials recovery facility in every barangay to regulate wastes collection at source and  reduce the volume of wastes at a manageable level  thus, minimize the cost of tipping fees; (b) institute the appropriate sanctions against all Private Persons including LGUs  using the  Open Dump Site  for unauthorized dumping of wastes; (c) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal; (d) provide the necessary protective personal equipment to all  personnel, whether LGU personnel  or hauler,  who are  directly involved in the operation of solid wastes management; (e) require the hauler to comply with the  Minimum Requirements for Establishing and Operating Transfer Stations and; (f) designate a MENRO to oversee and monitor the Municipality’s solid wastes management system and practices.

We recommended that the (a) BUB Focal Person of the respective programs of the LGU (i) strictly monitor the  implementation of targeted projects and take note of any gap for future inputs; (ii) determine reasons for  the non-receipt of the funds from the NGAs concerned in order to determine the next steps to be taken by  the LGU in case the project is  no longer viable; (b) MSWDO prepare the profiling of intended beneficiaries before the project proposal is finalized/submitted for approval of the LCE; and ; (c) Human Resources Management Officer to  augment  personnel in the MSWDO in order to update the databank of residents/ intended beneficiaries per category or classification as to economic condition for easy identification of program beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
a) Monitoring of implementation of projects and receipt of funds

b) Starting of profiling of intended beneficiaries

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with GSIS regulations on deduction of premium contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Continue to being compliant with GSIS regulations in the deduction of premiums and the timely remittances thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of BIR regulations on the remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Continue to being compliant with BIR regulations in the deduction of premiums and the timely remittances thereof.
Management has filed an appeal memorandum to the Office of the Chairman on the disallowance amounting to ₱13,114,000.00. The outcome of the appeal is still pending with the COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the accountant to take appropriate action to report these items that cannot be traced anymore and (b)  prepare a formal request to COA for the write-off of these dormant accounts to pave the way for the de-recognition and/or adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management explained there was no available documents to support the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management  (a) require the Budget Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Property-in-Charge to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.
observations.managementAction: Management included the appropriation for insurance coverage of properties in its 2017 Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget in compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities/programs for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (c) maximize utilization of SEF for the attainment of its primary goals and objectives and ensure that PPAs are in accordance with DepEd criteria and the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that  priority expenditures  will be included in its 2017 Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) observe strictly the DSWD regulations relative to the grant of financial assistance; and (b) request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing a set of specific guidelines, in harmony with DSWD policies, on the grant of financial assistance/aid to rationalize disbursements under the Other MOOE account.
observations.managementAction: Management commit to implement in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require (a) strict compliance with Section 361, Volume I of the GAAM to effectively control and monitor the use thereof and (b) regular and prompt submission to the Office of the Auditor of the required periodic reports for the timely evaluation of the reasonableness of the utilization of motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: For implementation in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) adopt the best environmental, economical, efficient and effective practice/s in ecological waste management;  and (b) effect the passage of an ordinance/s to  effectively enforce the mandatory segregation of wastes at source, the no segregation, no collection rule, and the use of environmentally-acceptable packaging by all business establishments.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the recommendations but explained that it will take some time to comply.

observations.recommendation: 1.7    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the  Inventory Committee to give utmost priority to the conduct of the required annual physical count of property; (b)  create a General Services Officer (GSO) position who will discharge on a full time basis the property custodianship functions and the receipt, issuance and recording of the procurement and issuance of  supplies and property; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile regularly her records with the Municipal Treasurer.

2.7       We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate disposal of the  14 unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations. Retrieve and determine the actual condition  of the four units of unserviceable, unutilized and abandoned solid waste  equipment found at the open dumpsite  and take appropriate action to either repair, rehabilitate or dispose them accordingly.

3.7  We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant to analyze, review and monitor all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded and that  dormant, long outstanding and unsubstantiated accounts are  properly  disposed  of and derecognized; and (b)  prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the write-off of these dormant accounts to pave the way for their derecognition and/or adjustment in the books of accounts.

4.6      We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Budget Officer to include in the budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Property Officer in-Charge to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable property with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

5.7   We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) effect the closure and transfer of the bank account being maintained with the  non-government depository bank within one year from the effectivity of Department Circular No. 002-2015 unless a Bureau of Local Government Finance approval has been secured; and (b) henceforth, comply with DOF Department Circular Nos. 001-2015 and 002-2015 in the deposit of funds of the Municipality.

6.15   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) stop the granting  of cash advances to DepEd officials and limit the granting of  cash advances only to the Municipality’s designated DO; (ii) demand the immediate liquidation of the unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱553,800.00 from the concerned officials; and; (iii) desist from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; (b) require the two District Supervisors to (i) submit the approved project proposals for the cash advances granted in September, 2016 totaling ₱373,750.00; (ii) refund the difference between the authorized and actual expenses incurred during the centralized training amounting to ₱70,800.00; and (iii) submit all required supporting travel documents to establish the validity of the travel in Tokyo, Japan or refund the amount spent in case of non-submission of the required documents.

7.6    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Budget Officer and the District Supervisor to stop the practice of charging inappropriate expenses to SEF which the DepEd is mandated to incur and pay out of its own appropriations; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from reimbursing expenses to District Supervisors which are not allowable using SEF.

8.6       We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to adopt public bidding as the general method of procurement and to limit the use of alternative mode of procurement to highly exceptional cases only as provided in the said IRR.

9.6     We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.

10.8    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) BAC officials to  religiously post all bid opportunities in the PhilGEPS website; (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers before allowing payment of claims thereof; and (c) Acting General Servicer Officer to maintain a centralized storage area and render necessary reports of issuances of medicines duly supported with RIS.

11.6   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal  Accountant  to desist from charging improper expenditures against the 20% development fund and that projects charged should  partake of the nature of investment or capital expenditures; (b) the Acting  General Service Officer to  ensure that no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP; and  (c) Bids and Award Committee post all  projects to be bidded/awarded  in the PhilGEPS website  in accordance with the pertinent provisions in the 2016  IRR of RA No.9184.

12.7   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a)  require the Municipal Accountant to make charges against  the GAD Fund to only  PPAs that are relevant and will address the identified gender issues and their causes; (b) ensure that the GAD  PPAs respond to the identified gender issues and concerns and that GAD mandates are implemented; (c) conduct gender analysis using the prescribed tools  to ensure that PPAs are gender responsive to  address the prevailing and recurring issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men stakeholders, clients and constituents; and  (d) monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs and prepare the AR in the prescribed format.

13.5     We reiterated our recommendation that Management  (a) require all users of Motor Vehicles to prepare and maintain the complete files of properly accomplished and duly approved Driver’s  Trip Tickets  pursuant to  Section 361, Volume I of the GAAM to effectively control and monitor the use of Motor Vehicles;  and (b) submit  to the Office of the Auditor the required periodic reports of Monthly Report of Official Travels  and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for the timely evaluation of the reasonableness of the utilization of motor vehicles and fuel consumption.

14.6   We recommended that the MDRRMO and the LDRRM Council take the necessary and timely recommendation to the SB for the declaration of a state of calamity if warranted by the prevailing conditions while providing  immediate response to disaster victims. In case there is a declaration of a state of calamity by the SB, the corresponding lifting under appropriate circumstances thereafter, shall also be issued.

15.8      We recommended that the MSWDO conduct  actual validation of the families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victims’ profiles containing updated and realistic data to become the basis in next year’s planning activities to ensure adequate  and appropriate procurement of  relief goods on a timely basis to properly address the needs of disaster victims.

16.6    We recommended that the concerned offices namely the MSWDO and the MDRRMO immediately inform the Municipal Treasurer or his  authorized representative on the existence of donations in-kind to properly acknowledge the receipt thereof. Likewise, proper reporting, accounting, and recording of accountability for donated goods should be made by the respective offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO and the BAC officials to undertake on a timely basis   the immediate  procurement of the necessary rescue equipment and vehicles  in accordance with the approved AIP for the year.

18.12  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) establish a materials recovery facility in every barangay to regulate wastes collection at source and  reduce the volume of wastes at a manageable level  thus, minimize the cost of tipping fees; (b)institute the appropriate sanctions against all Private Persons including LGUs  using the  Open Dump Site  for unauthorized dumping of wastes; (c) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal; (d) provide the necessary protective personal equipment to all  personnel, whether LGU personnel  or hauler,  who are  directly involved in the operation of solid wastes management; (e) require the hauler to comply with the  Minimum Requirements for Establishing and Operating Transfer Stations and; (f) designate a MENRO to oversee and monitor the Municipality’s solid wastes management system and practices.

19.10   We recommended that the (a) BUB Focal Person of the respective programs of the LGU (i) strictly monitor the  implementation of targeted projects and take note of any gap for future inputs; (ii) determine reasons for  the non-receipt of the funds from the NGAs concerned in order to determine the next steps to be taken by  the LGU in case the project is  no longer viable; (b) MSWDO prepare the profiling of intended beneficiaries before the project proposal is finalized/submitted for approval of the LCE; and ; (c) Human Resources Management Officer to  augment  personnel in the MSWDO in order to update the databank of residents/ intended beneficiaries per category or classification as to economic condition for easy identification of program beneficiaries.

20.4   We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with GSIS regulations on deduction of premium contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
21.4     We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of BIR regulations on the remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management comply strictly with the DOF regulation and secure authority therefrom should the Municipality retain its deposits with other banks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality closed its time deposit account with Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) create a General Services Officer (GSO) position who will handle the receipts, issuance and recording of the procured supplies and property; (b) form an Inventory Team who will devote time and attention to fast tract the conduct of a full-blown inventory of all the property of the Municipality and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile regularly her record with the Municipal Treasurer’s record.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Treasurer was designated as Property Custodian; 

(b) Inventory Team was already created

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management effect the disposal of the unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with existing rules and regulations in order to protect them from further deterioration and generate additional funds for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of required reports for the disposal of properties is now in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the BAC to submit copies of the perfected contracts within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, for the timely conduct by the Audit Team of the contract review and other necessary audit procedures over infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Submission of the requirements is being complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from paying the annual membership dues of other organizations not in consonance with Section 508 of RA 7160.

We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) desist from charging ineligible expenses against the 20% Development Fund and (b) plan programs/projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management admits the lack of strict monitoring on the charges made to the fund and agrees to comply in CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the LDRRM Officer/Municipal Planning and Development Officer to comply strictly with the reporting guidelines pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The required allocation was made but proper utilization was not strictly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to (a) prepare and implement its GAD Plan; (b) adequately allocate budget for it; and (c) prepare and submit accomplishment reports thereon to the agencies concerned, pursuant to Section 34 of the GAA for FY 2015.
observations.managementAction: Management agrees to observe the guidelines on GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant with GSIS regulations in the deduction of premiums and the timely remittances thereof.

We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the pertinent BIR regulations and direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the appropriate amount of withholding taxes intact to the BIR within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Management commits to strictly observe BIR regulations.
